title: Greek Myths Week 1
strapline: One startable day built from a creator-style curriculum stack
signal: Guide view transformed into a clear Player day
sourceSubtitle: A real-world pile of materials that becomes one clear day inside Lesson Hollow.

sourceItems.label: D'Aulaires' Book of Greek Myths
sourceItems.detail: Pages 18 to 27 for the Minotaur and Theseus thread

sourceItems.label: YouTube explainer clip
sourceItems.detail: One short overview of Theseus and the labyrinth

sourceItems.label: Sketchbook prompt
sourceItems.detail: Draw the labyrinth with a thick marker and strong turns

sourceItems.label: Map prompt
sourceItems.detail: Place Crete and Knossos on the world map

sourceItems.label: Narration note
sourceItems.detail: Say one thing Theseus did before writing a sentence

tiktok.hook: Take this Greek myths stack and turn it into a day a kid can actually start.
tiktok.caption: The source materials are good. The missing piece is a clean Today page that tells the learner what to do next.
beats: Show the book, video, and notebook prompts as a loose pile.
Cut to the Lesson Hollow Today page with a clear first task already marked done.
Slow-scroll the rest of the day so the viewer can feel the friction drop.
End on the line about turning a curriculum stack into a startable day.
tiktok.cta: Lesson Hollow turns a curriculum pile into a clear next step.

tasks.title: Watch one short video clip
tasks.note: 12-minute overview of Theseus and the labyrinth

tasks.title: Sketch the labyrinth
tasks.note: Use the notebook page with a thick black marker

tasks.title: Label Crete on the map
tasks.note: Add Knossos if there is time

tasks.title: Narrate one thing Theseus did
tasks.note: Say it out loud first, then write one sentence

tasks.title: Choose tomorrow's myth trail
tasks.note: Pick Medusa or Icarus based on what feels alive
